items.title: Approval Phishing: From Just One Case to Full-Scale Disruption
items.summary: Chain of Thought is our new expert-hosted webinar series, taking you behind the scenes of real investigations, emerging typologies and… The post Approval Phishing: From Just One Case to Full-Scale Disruption appeared first on Chainalysis.

items.title: US senators urge CFTC probe Polymarket over ‘deceptive marketing’
items.summary: Senators John Curtis and Adam Schiff are concerned about the CFTC’s enforcement ability after a “troubling” report on Polymarket’s advertising.

items.title: Too big to fail: Strategy’s $13 billion bitcoin paper loss alone dwarfs hundreds of prominent tokens
items.summary: Strategy’s paper loss exceeds the market caps of hundreds of tokens, highlighting the extreme concentration of risk in the crypto market right now.

items.title: SEC, CFTC seek input on unified portfolio margin rules across securities and derivatives
items.summary: The regulatory agencies are seeking public feedback on cross-margining, collateral and risk management as cryptocurrency derivatives and multi-asset trading continue to expand.

items.title: Bitcoin makes first sub-$60K close since Q3 2024 as tech stocks enter ‘deep bear market’
items.summary: Bitcoin risked turning $60,000 into resistance as BTC price weakness persisted following another tech-driven sell-off in Asian stock markets.

items.title: a16z-backed crypto firm rebrands, shifts focus to solving AI’s global copyright headache
items.summary: The startup formerly known as Story Protocol raised $140 million to secure internet rights and is now building an audit layer for data consent, licensing, and provenance for tech firms.

items.title: XRP risks drop below $1, but onchain data highlights silver lining
items.summary: XRP’s chance of a daily close below $1 are rising, but whale accumulation and shrinking exchange supply may be a sign that traders are buying.

items.title: Ripple CEO stays bullish on bitcoin but says Saylor's strategy has hurt crypto
items.summary: Ripple's Brad Garlinghouse called Strategy's preferred-stock funding model "financial engineering" that distracted the market, pointing to STRC's slide to a record low as the evidence. He runs the company behind XRP, a bitcoin rival.

items.title: Dogecoin and Hyperliquid's HYPE led weekly crypto losses as AI stocks lure buyers
items.summary: A rotation out of chipmakers lifted much of the stock market, with the equal-weight S&P 500 at a record. Crypto was not part of it. Ether fell 8% on the week and the memecoins dropped harder.

items.title: Securitize expects to raise $400M ahead of public debut
items.summary: Securitize says less than 30% of shareholders in the acquisition firm taking it public elected to redeem, giving it an expected $400 million on its upcoming debut.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: SOL reclaims $72, but onchain data flags weakening momentum
items.summary: Solana found strength from tokenized stock trading on its network, but a declining TVL and DEX volumes point to fading momentum.

items.title: Aave, Solana ecosystem tokens lead crypto rebound as bitcoin steadies near $60,000
items.summary: Tokenized stock trading fueled fresh momentum across the Solana ecosystem, while Aave founder hinted at token buybacks coming under new framework.

items.title: U.S. House Democrat, who may soon run key committee, condemns crypto in 401(k)s
items.summary: Maxine Waters, the ranking Democrat on House Financial Services, asked the chief of the Department of Labor to withdraw its proposal on alternative assets.

items.title: Former Ethereum Foundation leader warns of funding gap as governance shifts
items.summary: A former Ethereum Foundation member says the network must quickly build new funding institutions as the Foundation steps back.

items.title: Anti-trafficking group says Clarity Act's Section 604 could weaken accountability
items.summary: An anti-human trafficking advocate said a Clarity Act provision could weaken accountability despite existing criminal laws.

items.title: Virtuals' Jansen Teng says AI agents are evolving into autonomous economic actors
items.summary: The next phase of AI agents may be less about chat and more about earning, spending and coordinating.

items.title: Securitize expects to raise $400 million as tokenization firm nears public debut
items.summary: The BlackRock-backed tokenization specialist expects to close its SPAC merger next week and start trading on the NYSE, pending shareholder approval.

items.title: CoinDesk 20 performance update: AAVE jumps 8.9%, leading index higher
items.summary: Solana (SOL) gained 4.5%, joining Aave (AAVE) as a top performer.

items.title: Wall Street's IPO revival hasn't reached dot-com euphoria levels, Goldman Sachs says
items.summary: U.S. IPO issuance has rebounded sharply in 2026, but the bank said the current surge lacks the deal volume and speculative excess that defined the dot-com era.
